Title: Functional roles of LaeA, polyketide synthase, and glucose oxidase in the regulation of ochratoxin A biosynthesis and virulence in Aspergillus carbonarius

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acidifies infected fruit tissue and contributes to fungal virulence during fruit colonization
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colonized nectarine and grape berry tissue; YES medium
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during fruit colonization; reduced in ΔAclaeA and increased in ΔAcpks backgrounds
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in infected nectarine tissue at day 5 postinoculation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene knockout; RT-qPCR; gluconic acid quantification; fruit infection/virulence assays; pH measurement
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit colonization of Sun Snow nectarine and Zani grape berries; YES medium at pH 4.0
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gluconic acid accumulation and contributes positively to virulence; knockout abolishes gluconic acid production and reduces decay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Acgox disruption completely eliminated gluconic acid production in vitro and in infected fruit. 2. ΔAcgox showed reduced virulence on nectarines and grapes. 3. ΔAcgox moderately reduced OTA accumulation and downregulated laeA and OTA cluster genes. 4. Loss of Acgox shifted organic acid output toward citric acid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion redirects carbon flux toward citric acid accumulation; tissue acidification can still occur despite loss of gluconic acid
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although GOX affects OTA-associated gene expression, its main metabolism-relevant validated role here is control of gluconic acid production rather than direct catalysis of OTA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fungal iterative type I polyketide synthase
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Polyketide intermediate for ochratoxin A biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of the polyketide moiety required for ochratoxin A biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor required for target product biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Food/feed contaminant with adverse effects on human and animal health
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colonized nectarine and grape berry tissue; YES medium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in infected fruit in WT; absent in ΔAcpks mutant
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in infected nectarine tissue at day 5 postinoculation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene knockout; HPLC-based OTA quantification; RT-qPCR; fruit infection assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit colonization of Sun Snow nectarine and Zani grape berries; YES medium at pH 4.0
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for OTA biosynthesis; knockout completely abolishes OTA production but does not reduce virulence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Acpks knockout eliminated detectable OTA production in vitro and in fruit. 2. ΔAcpks showed colonization and virulence similar to or slightly greater than WT. 3. Acpks loss downregulated laeA and other OTA cluster genes. 4. OTA accumulation is therefore not required for pathogenicity in the tested fruit cultivars.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of Acpks may increase gluconic acid accumulation and slightly increase decay area; OTA-negative phenotype may not impair pathogenic growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper interprets PKS as a key component of the OTA biosynthetic cluster; exact PKS reaction chemistry is not experimentally dissected in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting Acpks is a plausible strategy to eliminate OTA contamination in fruit-associated A. carbonarius without necessarily reducing fungal growth.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Global regulator of secondary metabolism; transcriptional regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LaeA family; putative methyltransferase-domain-containing global regulator
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ochratoxin A is a harmful food/feed mycotoxin; gluconic acid acidifies host tissue and promotes virulence
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colonized nectarine and grape berry tissue; YES medium
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary metabolite biosynthesis and organic-acid-associated acidification during infection
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OTA biosynthetic cluster genes: bZIP transcription factor; Acpks; nrps; p450; hal; Acgox
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/positively regulates expression of OTA cluster genes and Acgox
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit colonization of Sun Snow nectarine and Zani grape berries; YES medium at pH 4.0
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in WT during infection; reduced in ΔAcpks and ΔAcgox backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in infected nectarine tissue at day 5 postinoculation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene knockout; RT-qPCR of target genes; OTA quantification; gluconic acid measurement; fruit infection assays; pH measurement
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes OTA biosynthesis, gluconic acid accumulation, sporulation, conidial germination, and virulence; deletion strongly reduces OTA and attenuates disease
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of AclaeA drastically reduced OTA accumulation in vitro and in colonized fruit. 2. ΔAclaeA downregulated all five OTA-cluster genes and Acgox. 3. Loss of AclaeA reduced gluconic acid accumulation, impaired tissue acidification, and attenuated virulence on nectarines and grapes. 4. ΔAclaeA also reduced sporulation and delayed conidial germination.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator required for strong OTA biosynthesis and for full gluconic-acid-associated virulence during fruit infection
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduces fungal developmental traits such as sporulation and germination in addition to metabolite outputs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Interfering with LaeA-regulated networks may reduce OTA contamination and virulence, but broad pleiotropic effects should be expected.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper refers to LaeA as a transcriptional factor/global regulator of secondary metabolism; direct DNA binding was inferred from target gene expression changes rather than shown biochemically.
